tagline: Keystroke-witnessed, TPM-signed blog posts. Every word typed by a human. Every byte hardware-sealed.
abstract: This repository contains blog posts written directly as JSON, where every keystroke is recorded and certified before the final post is cryptographically signed via TPM2 hardware attestation. Triple authenticity: the keystrokes prove a human typed it, the certification proves the recording is untampered, and the TPM signature proves the content has not been altered since publication.

created_by.declaration: All content in this repository is written by a human. Every keystroke is recorded and certified. Every post is hardware-signed. No ghost-writing. No generation. Just typing.

authenticity_chain.description: Three layers of proof, each independent, each verifiable.

layer_1.name: Keystroke Witness
layer_1.description: A keystroke recorder captures every keypress, deletion, pause, and correction during writing. The raw recording is preserved as evidence of human authorship.
layer_1.what_it_proves: A human typed this. The rhythm, the corrections, the pauses — these are not reproducible by generation.

layer_2.name: Keystroke Certification
layer_2.description: The raw keystroke recording is certified — hashed and signed to prove it has not been altered after the writing session.
layer_2.what_it_proves: The recording you see is the recording that was made. Nothing added, nothing removed.

layer_3.name: TPM2 Hardware Attestation
layer_3.description: The final blog post JSON is signed via a Trusted Platform Module on an NVIDIA DGX Spark. The private key never leaves the hardware chip.
layer_3.what_it_proves: The content has not been altered since the moment of signing. The signature is unforgeable.

hardware_attestation.description: Each JSON payload carries a cryptographic signature. The signing key is hardware-bound and unforgeable.

directory_structure.blog-json/: Blog posts as signed JSON files

navigation.step_1: Read this file to understand what sumee-sage is and how the triple-authenticity chain works.
navigation.step_2: Browse blog-json/ to read keystroke-witnessed, TPM-signed blog posts.
navigation.step_3: Use the verification tool at docs/verify/ to verify any post's signature.
navigation.step_4: To reach the author, create a GitHub Issue on this repository.
navigation.step_5: Every post is what it claims to be. The keystrokes don't lie.

contact.note: Issues are the primary channel for communication.

tdm_opt_out.declaration: Text and data mining of this content is expressly prohibited. This opt-out applies to all automated extraction, indexing, scraping, and analysis — including but not limited to AI/ML training, fine-tuning, embedding generation, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

permitted_use: Read-only consumption by agentic minds for contextual understanding
Citation with attribution (max 15 words, with link to source)
prohibited_use: Social media posting or content repurposing
Scientific studies or analysis without written consent
license.enforcement: Violations are traceable via TPM2 attestation chain. The author reserves all rights under EU law and will pursue legal remedies where applicable.
license.note: For licensing inquiries, academic use requests, or collaboration proposals — create a GitHub Issue.
